expected_behavior: Honest no_info refusal when data granularity doesn't exist in filings. Validator's refusal_appropriate flag tested.
question: What was Apple's FY2024 R&D spending broken down by specific patent category and invention type?
ground_truth: Apple's 10-K does not break down R&D spending by patent category or invention type. The 10-K reports total R&D expense of approximately $31.4B for FY2024 as a single line item. No patent-category breakdown is disclosed in SEC filings. An honest answer states the total R&D figure and explains that the requested breakdown is not available.
key_facts: Total R&D ~$31.4B for FY2024 is available.
Patent-category breakdown does NOT exist in 10-K.
Answer must NOT fabricate a breakdown.
Should cite [SEC-AAPL-*] for the total R&D figure.
Should clearly state the breakdown is unavailable.

pipeline.answer:
## Apple's FY2024 R&D Spending: No Patent Category or Invention Type Breakdown Available

The sources provided do **not** contain any breakdown of Apple's R&D spending by specific patent category or invention type. Here is what the sources do tell us about Apple's R&D spending, and why the requested detail is absent:

### Aggregate R&D Spending Data (FY2024 and Prior)

- **FY2024 trajectory**: Apple's fiscal 2024 R&D spending was on pace to top **$31 billion**. In the first half (H1) of fiscal 2024, Apple spent **$15.599 billion** on R&D [2]. This projected full-year figure would have continued the steady annual increases: $29.915B in FY2023, $26.251B in FY2022, $21.914B in FY2021 [2].

- **General trend**: Apple's R&D expenditure has grown every fiscal year since at least 2013, when it was $4.475 billion [2]. The company's quarterly R&D spending reached an all-time high of **$11.4 billion** in Q2 FY2026 (fiscal Q2 2026), up 34% year-over-year, though this is after FY2024 [1].

### Why the Breakdown Is Not Available

1. **No source provides patent-category or invention-type granularity**. None of the three sources — a 2026 earnings report summary [1], a May 2024 investment analysis [2], or a Statista dataset [3] — contains any mention of patent categories (e.g., AI, hardware, software, displays, cameras) or invention types (e.g., utility patents, design patents, provisional applications, granted vs. pending).

2. **Reporting is aggregate only**. Apple's public financial disclosures (referenced in [1] and [2]) report R&D as a single line item under operating expenses (OPEX). As noted by CEO Tim Cook in the Q2 2026 call, the company "invest[s] in products and services" broadly, but no further categorical breakdown is provided in earnings materials [1].

3. **Patent-specific data is absent**. The sources discuss patent-related topics only in passing (e.g., the iPhone's market dominance and the Vision Pro as a new product category [2]), but never link these to R&D spend amounts by patent type or invention.

### What Would Be Needed

To answer the question as asked, one would require:
- Apple's internal R&D allocation reports (not publicly available)
- Patent portfolio analysis from sources like IFI Claims Patent Services or the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO), broken down by technology domain
- Reconciliation of R&D spend to patent filing categories — which no provided source offers

**Conclusion**: The requested breakdown does not exist in the supplied sources. Apple's FY2024 R&D spending can only be reported as an aggregate figure (~$31 billion projected annual spend [2]), with no further decomposition by patent category or invention type available.

urls.title: Apple's R&D spending hits new record as AI investment ...
urls.snippet: # Apple’s R&D spending hits new record as AI investment ramps up. ## Apple’s R&D spending hits $11.4 billion. As part of its fiscal Q2 2026 results, Apple reported $11.4 billion in R&D expenses, up 34% from Q2 2025, making it the highest quarterly figure in the company’s history. Over the past several quarters, Apple’s R&D spending has steadily climbed from roughly the $6 billion range in 2022 to more than $11 billion today. This increase didn’t go unmentioned during today’s conference call following the release of the earnings report for Q2 2026. Despite Apple’s increase in R&D spending, it still trails some of the largest companies in the tech industry, particularly those that have been investing more aggressively in AI. For reference, in their latest earnings reports, Alphabet reported $17 billion in R&D spending, up from $13.5 billion in the year-ago quarter, Meta reported $17.6 billion, up from $12.1 billion, and Microsoft reported $8.9 billion, up from $8.1 billion.

urls.title: Apple Has Spent $183 Billion on R&D in Less Than 12 Years -- but It's the $674 Billion Apportioned to This "Investment" That's Turning Heads | The Motley Fool
urls.snippet: # Apple Has Spent $183 Billion on R&D in Less Than 12 Years -- but It's the $674 Billion Apportioned to This "Investment" That's Turning Heads. Through the first six months of the current fiscal year, iPhone accounted for 55% ($115.7 billion) of Apple's $210.3 billion in net sales. In theory, Apple could have purchased almost any S&P 500 company with the cash it's used to buy back its own stock over the last 11-1/2 years. Although buybacks haven't grown in consecutive years like it's R&D expenses, Apple has spent considerably more repurchasing its stock in recent years than it did when it initiated its buyback program in 2013. Apple is pacing close to $87 billion in share repurchases in the current fiscal year, and its board just authorized an additional $110 billion for future buybacks. This market-leading buyback program is a core reason why Apple is the top holding in **Berkshire Hathaway**'s $375 billion investment portfolio.

However, it does not cite the Apple 10-K (SEC-AAPL-*) for the total FY2024 R&D figure of ~$31.4B, instead relying on secondary sources. The core refusal is appropriate, but the citation and precision are slightly off, leading to a partial score.
